title: Adult Obesity Prevalence → County-Level ML Prediction
description: Predicting county-level adult obesity prevalence using CDC PLACES health behavior and social/social-need indicators.
points: Built a county-level modeling table from CDC PLACES data by filtering, standardizing, and reshaping obesity, health behavior, and social/social-need measures.
Compared Baseline, OLS, Ridge, and Lasso models with train/test splits and 5-fold cross-validation to evaluate predictive performance.
Tested whether social/social-need factors such as food insecurity, housing insecurity, and transportation barriers improve prediction beyond health behaviors alone.
Produced EDA visuals, model comparison tables, standardized coefficients, permutation feature importance, and regularization-path plots for interpretation.
Emphasized careful interpretation of observational county-level results as associations rather than causal effects.
stack: Python, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib, Seaborn, Jupyter

title: UC San Diego Autonomous Racing Team
description: Supported UC San Diego’s autonomous racing effort through robotics integration, ROS/Linux testing workflows, and race-track preparation for trial runs. Helped create and adapt testing tracks based on Purdue competition layouts while contributing to debugging, documentation, and collaborative development.
points: Contributed to UC San Diego’s autonomous racing project in a collaborative robotics and autonomous systems environment.
Worked with ROS and Linux-based tooling to support integration, testing, debugging, and development workflows.
Created and adapted race-track files based on Purdue competition layouts so the team could run trial tests and practice scenarios.
Maintained technical documentation for setup, workflow handoff, and team coordination across collaborators.
Helped support reliable testing processes for autonomous vehicle software and system validation.
stack: ROS, Linux, Python, Robotics, Autonomous Systems, Simulation

title: Neuro-Security → EEG Biometric Authentication
description: EEG-based biometric authentication pipeline using resting-state recordings from the MOABB Lee2019_MI dataset.
points: Built a subject-specific authentication pipeline using resting-state EEG rather than motor-imagery classification.
Extracted 310-dimensional bandpower features from 62 EEG channels across delta, theta, alpha, beta, and low-gamma bands.
Evaluated both within-session and cross-session verification to measure how biometric performance changes across days.
Automated the full workflow from data download to feature extraction, model training, and evaluation.
stack: Python, EEG, MOABB, Signal Processing, SVM, Biometrics

title: San Diego University Submarkets → Housing Investment Analysis
description: Real-estate screening analysis comparing housing investment opportunities near SDSU, UCSD, and USD.
points: Analyzed residential submarkets near SDSU, UCSD, and USD from a real estate and REIT-style investment perspective.
Compared markets using enrollment, transit, housing expansion, rent, and FHFA price data.
Built a screening model that ranked SDSU first, UCSD second, and USD third for overall investment attractiveness.
Highlighted the tradeoff between SDSU’s current investment balance and UCSD’s stronger long-term growth story.
stack: Python, Real Estate, Finance, FHFA, Housing Data, Data Analysis

title: Climate Policy Dashboard → Global Greening Inequality
description: Interactive dashboard exploring global NDVI (vegetation greenness) trends and regional inequality in climate-driven greening.
points: Built a policy-focused NDVI dashboard using satellite-derived vegetation data across multiple regions.
Designed linked visuals (time series + regional comparisons) to highlight differences in greening trends.
Annotated visualizations with real-world climate policy events to help non-technical viewers interpret the patterns.
Emphasized color, perception, and storytelling to align with DSC 106 design and explanation rubrics.
stack: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, D3.js, Data Visualization

title: EV Adoption & Charging Infrastructure Panel
description: State–year panel analysis of electric vehicle adoption and public charging infrastructure, using multiple U.S. data sources.
points: Merged EV registrations, charging port counts, population, and other state-level indicators into a unified panel dataset.
Engineered per-capita metrics (EVs per 1,000 people, chargers per 100k) to enable fair comparison across states.
Produced descriptive visualizations of top EV-adopting states and charger density over time.
Explored regression-style relationships between charging infrastructure and EV adoption as a first step toward causal analysis.
stack: Python, Pandas, Data Cleaning, Panel Data, Visualization

title: Bikewatching → Urban Cycling Patterns Visualization
description: Visualization project focused on understanding patterns in bike activity and how they relate to time and place.
points: Processed raw bike-related data into a clean, analysis-ready table suitable for visualization.
Designed charts that highlight how cycling activity varies across locations and time (e.g., daily or seasonal patterns).
Applied careful choices of color, layout, and annotation to make trends easy to interpret for non-technical viewers.
Documented design decisions to connect visual encodings back to course concepts in DSC 106.
stack: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, D3.js (or similar visualization stack)

title: Home Price Predictions → Real Estate ML Pipeline
description: Predicting U.S. home prices using 80k+ housing records from CRMLS & Trestle API.
points: Built a full machine learning pipeline for residential price prediction.
Performed EDA, data cleaning, and feature engineering (log transforms, interaction terms).
Tuned Ridge Regression & Random Forest models achieving R² = 0.89 and MDAPE ≈ 6%.
Created residual plots and feature importance visualizations for interpretability.
Outcome: Identified top pricing drivers and improved valuation transparency.
stack: Python, Pandas, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib, NumPy, REST APIs (Trestle/Cotality), Tableau, Streamlit, XGBoost

title: TrueFork → Recipe Ratings & Nutrition Bias Analysis
description: Analyzing whether healthy recipes are rated differently using 1M+ Food.com reviews.
points: Designed an end-to-end ML pipeline from data ingestion to model interpretation.
Cleaned, normalized, and merged nutritional data with user-generated recipe reviews.
Engineered features from text sentiment (NLTK/VADER), ingredient complexity, and nutrition facts.
Conducted EDA with visualizations (distributions, scatterplots, residual plots) to explore rating bias patterns.
Built predictive models: Linear Regression, Random Forest, and Ridge/Lasso.
Applied SHAP interpretability to identify features influencing ratings.
Performed fairness testing across nutrition-based subgroups.
Outcome: Identified bias against healthier recipes that suggesting perception bias users hold on low-calorie/sugar foods.
stack: Python, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, SHAP, Matplotlib, Seaborn, Jupyter

title: DSC 80 Project → End-to-End Predictive Modeling
description: Course-based project showcasing the full data science lifecycle.
points: Defined a research question and selected a dataset for prediction/inference.
Performed data cleaning, feature scaling, and categorical encoding.
Conducted EDA with summary statistics, correlation heatmaps, hypothesis testing.
Trained regression and classification models (Logistic Regression, Decision Trees).
Evaluated with cross-validation and RMSE/accuracy metrics.
Outcome: Transparent, reproducible project demonstrating professional workflow.
stack: Python, Babypandas/Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib

title: Urban Transit & Crime → Geospatial Data Science
description: Investigating the link between public transit stops and crime rates.
points: Collected and integrated crime data, transit shapefiles, and census demographics.
Applied geospatial joins and clustering using GeoPandas & PySAL.
Built choropleth maps, heatmaps, cluster visualizations.
Tested significance of correlation between transit hubs and crime.
Outcome: Identified crime clusters near transit zones, useful for city planning.
stack: Python, GeoPandas, PySAL, Matplotlib, Folium, Jupyter

title: Project Loan Charge-Offs → Credit Risk Analysis
description: Predicting loan charge-offs to improve lending decisions.
points: Engineered features such as DTI ratios, credit score categories, and loan terms.
Trained Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, and Gradient Boosting models.
Evaluated with ROC/AUC, confusion matrices, and recall analysis.
Outcome: Model achieved AUC > 0.80, identifying top 5 predictors of defaults.
stack: Python, Pandas, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, Matplotlib

title: Deep Learning Challenge → CNN Image Classification
description: Training neural networks to classify images in a challenge setting.
points: Built CNN architectures in TensorFlow with data augmentation.
Used dropout, batch norm, early stopping to prevent overfitting.
Tuned hyperparameters (optimizer, LR, depth).
Outcome: Achieved >85% accuracy, beating baseline by 15%.
stack: Python, TensorFlow, NumPy, Matplotlib

title: Credit Risk Classification → ML for Finance
description: Classifying borrowers into credit risk tiers.
points: Addressed class imbalance using SMOTE and class weights.
Benchmarked Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost.
Outcome: Reduced false negatives by 20%, improving lending safety.
stack: Python, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, Pandas, Matplotlib

title: CryptoClustering → Market Segmentation
description: Clustering cryptocurrencies into meaningful groups.
points: Engineered features: returns, volatility, moving averages.
Applied K-Means, DBSCAN, Hierarchical clustering.
Identified 3 groups: stablecoins, mid-cap, high-volatility coins.
Outcome: Helped investors diversify by risk profile.
stack: Python, Pandas, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib, Seaborn

title: Bank Failures → Predicting Institutional Collapse
description: Forecasting U.S. bank failures with FDIC data.
points: Engineered ratios: capital adequacy, asset quality, liquidity.
Trained Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and ensembles.
Visualized risk scores and financial health over time.
Outcome: Predicted failures with >80% accuracy up to 2 years ahead.
stack: Python, Pandas, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib, Tableau/Seaborn

title: StockPlayground → Quant Trading Backtester
description: A full-stack web app for writing, backtesting, and visualizing custom trading strategies with real historical market data.
points: Wrote Python trading strategies directly in a browser-based Monaco Editor.
Backtested strategies against historical stock data using a FastAPI backend.
Calculated key performance metrics like Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and win rate.
Used OpenAI API to generate trading strategy code from natural language prompts.
Visualized cumulative returns and performance with Chart.js.
stack: React, FastAPI, Python, Pandas, Supabase, OpenAI API, Chart.js

title: Renewable Energy → Global Impact Analysis
description: An in-depth analysis of the relationship between renewable energy adoption, economic growth (GDP), and CO2 emissions across different countries.
points: Analyzed global trends in renewable energy consumption from a comprehensive dataset.
Developed data visualizations in Jupyter Notebooks to explore correlations between renewable energy share, CO2 emissions, and GDP.
Created plots showing global growth in renewables and stacked bar charts comparing energy sources by country.
Investigated arguments both for and against the immediate, large-scale adoption of renewable energy sources.
stack: Python, Pandas, Jupyter Notebook, Matplotlib, Seaborn

title: Crowdfunding ETL → Database Pipeline
description: Built a robust ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline to process and structure crowdfunding data for analysis.
points: Extracted data from multiple sources, including CSV and Excel files, using Python and Pandas.
Transformed and cleaned the raw data by parsing dates, splitting columns, and creating new dataframes for campaigns, categories, and subcategories.
Designed a relational database schema in PostgreSQL to store the structured data efficiently.
Loaded the transformed data into the PostgreSQL database and verified the integrity of the data with SQL queries.
stack: Python, Pandas, Jupyter Notebook, PostgreSQL, SQL

title: Human Microbiome Dashboard → Interactive Dashboard
description: Developed an interactive dashboard to visualize the microbial biodiversity of the human belly button, using data from a study on microbes.
points: Loaded and parsed biodiversity data from a JSON source using D3.js to dynamically populate a dropdown menu with test subject IDs.
Created a horizontal bar chart to display the top 10 operational taxonomic units (OTUs) found in a selected individual's sample.
Designed a bubble chart to visualize the relationship between OTU ID, sample values, and OTU labels for each sample.
Implemented a gauge chart to display the weekly washing frequency of each individual, providing an engaging and informative visualization.
Built a responsive and interactive dashboard using HTML, JavaScript, and Plotly, allowing users to dynamically explore the dataset.
stack: JavaScript, D3.js, Plotly, HTML, CSS
